research.summary: The Joon Shop is a real ecommerce brand selling Joon Solid Perfume, a Korean-made, alcohol-free, vegan solid fragrance positioned as perfume that is good for your skin. The brand is built around a focused four-SKU offer: three individual 6g solid perfumes and a complete collection set.
research.essence: Fragrance made intimate: a Korean-formulated solid perfume that melts into skin, travels everywhere, and turns scent into a quiet daily ritual.
research.aesthetic: Warm off-white, cream, stone, and neutral vanity-like surfaces; warm-neutral low-contrast grade with creamy highlights; sparse styling with one hero product, hands, pouch, fabric, bedside, handbag, or skincare-counter context; soft diffused daylight or large softbox from the side/front to reveal metal casing and balm texture without glare; close crops on fingers, wrists, hair tips, opened product, and product-in-hand; mix of clean centered product-only ecommerce shots and application moments with models or hands.

photoRules.grade: Shoot on warm off-white, cream, stone, and softly reflective neutral surfaces that feel like a bathroom counter, vanity, hotel bedside table, or editorial beauty still life. Use warm-neutral, low-contrast colour grading with creamy highlights and natural hydrated skin. Keep styling low to medium density: one hero product, a hand, soft fabric, pouch, bedside detail, handbag context, or restrained ingredient cue. Light with soft diffused daylight or a large softbox from the front/side so the metal casing catches gentle highlights and the balm texture is visible without hard glare. Crop close on wrists, fingers, hair tips, opened product, and product-in-hand; balance clean centered product-only pack shots with application moments because the swipe ritual is the story.

intelligence.overview: The Joon Shop is an Indian-owned ecommerce fragrance brand selling Korean-made solid perfumes. Its core idea is to make fragrance feel less like a volatile spray and more like a skin-friendly, portable, moisturising daily ritual.
intelligence.purpose: To make fragrance tactile, portable, discreet, and compatible with daily self-care rather than limited to alcohol-based sprays.
intelligence.mission: To create clean, alcohol-free solid perfumes that can be worn every day on pulse points and hair tips, with a buttery texture, skin-friendly ingredients, and easy reapplication throughout the day.
intelligence.vision: To normalize solid perfume as a modern, premium, handbag-friendly fragrance format in India and make it a credible replacement or companion to liquid perfume.
intelligence.story: Joon describes its origin as a search for a truly solid solid perfume. After trying existing formats and not finding the right one, the team found a perfumery and paired it with a Korean cosmetic chemist; after multiple formula versions and a year of internal use, Joon Solid Perfume was launched. The name Joon is described by the brand as meaning soul, spirit, and life.
intelligence.positioning: A Korean-made, skin-friendly solid perfume for people who want fragrance to feel as soft, portable, and ritualistic as skincare.
intelligence.audience: Urban Indian beauty and fragrance consumers who want a premium but practical scent format: fragrance beginners, clean beauty shoppers, Korean beauty followers, gift buyers, frequent travellers, people who dislike alcohol-heavy sprays, and anyone who wants discreet reapplication at work, in transit, or before going out.
intelligence.persona: Ananya is 29 and lives in Delhi, Mumbai, or Bangalore. She works in fashion, media, consulting, design, hospitality, or a founder-adjacent creative role, moving between office, cabs, gym, dinner, and weekend travel. Her bag always has lip balm, hand cream, compact, a mini comb, and one small luxury that makes her feel put together. She likes smelling good but does not want to spray perfume loudly in public or overpower people in close spaces; Joon works because it is discreet, tactile, non-spill, moisturising, aesthetically giftable, and easy to swipe on at a desk or in a car.
intelligence.toneOfVoice: Minimal, intimate, practical, clean, benefit-led, and self-assured.

typography.display: Clean modern sans serif, medium weight, unfussy, with restrained hierarchy.
typography.text: Legible ecommerce sans serif with direct skincare-like product copy.
typography.note: More Muji and Korean beauty counter than Parisian perfume house; functional, calm, and minimal rather than expressive or ornate.

intelligence.logoSystem: The brand appears wordmark-led, using Joon and The Joon Shop as the primary identity rather than an emblem-heavy system. The name carries the emotional meaning of soul, spirit, and life, while the product format and compact casing do much of the recognisable brand work.
intelligence.photographyStyle: Shoot on warm off-white, cream, stone, and softly reflective neutral surfaces that feel like a bathroom counter, vanity, hotel bedside table, or editorial beauty still life. Use warm-neutral, low-contrast colour grading with creamy highlights and natural hydrated skin. Keep styling low to medium density: one hero product, a hand, soft fabric, pouch, bedside detail, handbag context, or restrained ingredient cue. Light with soft diffused daylight or a large softbox from the front/side so the metal casing catches gentle highlights and the balm texture is visible without hard glare. Crop close on wrists, fingers, hair tips, opened product, and product-in-hand; balance clean centered product-only pack shots with application moments because the swipe ritual is the story.
intelligence.packagingStyle: Compact, premium, minimal, and handbag-ready, closer to skincare or colour cosmetics than traditional perfume bottles. Press describes the casing as chic and lipstick-like metal, reinforcing the idea of a fragrance object that can be held, opened, swiped, and reapplied.
intelligence.visualIdentity: Restrained cosmetic minimalism: cream and white grounds, near-black typography, skin-neutral beige, taupe shadow, and brushed silver casing. The world avoids bright perfume-category colour coding and instead signals clean beauty, Korean formulation, intimate ritual, and premium practicality.

insights: The format is the brand: the strongest asset is the physical behaviour of open, swipe, melt, carry, and refresh.
Good for your skin is sharper than solid perfume because it turns the format from novelty into a reason to care.
The brand should sell rituals, not just scents: desk refresh, post-commute, pre-dinner, flight bag, bedside, hair tips, and gifting drawer.
Korean formulation provides credibility, but intimacy is the emotional engine; this is a private fragrance you apply to yourself, not a cloud you spray into a room.
The product is naturally gender-flexible and should avoid becoming over-feminised; the stronger territory is close-to-skin modern fragrance.
Press already validates the trend, so the opportunity is to become the brand people name first when explaining solid perfume in India.
